summary: Iteration 23 - Tier/Price Consistency Testing PASSED. All 16 backend tests passed. All fixes verified working: Michael B. Jordan correctly B-LIST (55 langs, nominated not won Oscar), Daniel Radcliffe A-LIST (101 langs), Rochelle Humes C-LIST (13 langs), Pink (singer) A-LIST (78 langs) accessible via 'pink' alias. Category cards endpoint recalculates tier/price from Wikidata. Hot celebs endpoint returns correct tiers.

GET /api/autocomplete?q=pink - Returns Pink (singer) with A-LIST, £12M, 78 languages (CORRECT)
POST /api/celebrity/search - Michael B. Jordan returns B-LIST (CORRECT)
GET /api/hot-celebs - Returns 9 celebrities with correct tier/price
GET /api/hot-celebs - All celebrities have consistent tier/price
GET /api/celebrities/category/movie_stars - Returns celebrities with recalculated tier/price from Wikidata
GET /api/celebrities/category/musicians - Returns celebrities with recalculated tier/price from Wikidata
Award detection - Michael B. Jordan NOT A-LIST (nominated but not won Oscar)
Tier/price consistency - Daniel Radcliffe same tier across autocomplete and search
Tier/price consistency - Michael B. Jordan same tier across autocomplete and search
critical_code_review_comments: All tier/price consistency fixes verified working
calculate_tier_and_price() function correctly uses language count as primary indicator
major_award_patterns correctly requires WINNER keywords (not just 'academy award')
get_tier_and_price_from_wikidata() used consistently across all endpoints
Category endpoint correctly recalculates tier/price for each celebrity
context_for_next_testing_agent: All tier/price consistency fixes verified working in iteration 23. Key tier ranges: A-LIST (60+ langs), B-LIST (25-59 langs), C-LIST (10-24 langs), D-LIST (<10 langs). Achievement modifiers can upgrade tier by 1 level for franchise leads, award WINNERS (not nominees), and world champions. Test file: test_iteration_23_tier_price_consistency.py
